mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are positive in fewer than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) crosses above 3.0%, restrictive monetary policy sharply raises discount rates and debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns during periods when FEDFUNDS is above 3.0% are negative in fewer than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous presence of CPIAUCSL above 275 and FEDFUNDS below 0.5% signals an unsustainably loose real monetary stance that forces a rapid hawkish repricing, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns during joint trigger periods are negative in fewer than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over main effects (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1600 thousand units signals that interest-sensitive demand is rolling over, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as construction and durable goods sectors contract.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative in > 55% of instances when HOUST is below 1600 and UNRATE is below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) above 102 reflects manufacturing operating at tight capacity, intensifying input cost pressures that compress margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns when INDPRO exceeds 102 are negative in fewer than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) sustained above 620000 million dollars indicates robust consumer demand that supports top-line revenue growth across consumer-facing sectors, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following RSAFS levels above 620000 are not significantly positive versus baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 0.5% reduces the opportunity cost of equity capital and compresses risk-free yields, channeling capital into equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% do not exceed the unconditional mean return with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of unemployment below 4.0% (UNRATE) and retail sales above 600000 (RSAFS) signals an overheated economy with imminent policy intervention, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns during joint trigger periods are negative in fewer than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over main effects (F-test p > 0.05).
